recipes.title: Nihari
recipes.description: Slow-cooked spiced beef shank stew thickened with wheat flour and finished with ginger and chilies.
steps: Heat ghee and fry sliced onions until deep golden, then add ginger garlic paste.
Add beef shank and brown on all sides.
Stir in nihari masala and salt, cooking 2 minutes.
Add 2 liters of water, bring to a boil, then simmer covered for 4 hours until meat is fork tender.
Whisk wheat flour with cold water and stir into the stew to thicken.
Simmer another 30 minutes, stirring occasionally so it does not stick.
Serve garnished with julienned ginger, green chilies, lemon juice, and naan.

recipes.title: Haleem
recipes.description: Hearty pounded stew of meat, lentils, and wheat slow-cooked to a rich, thick porridge consistency.
steps: Soak cracked wheat and mixed lentils for 4 hours.
Cook beef with one onion and ginger garlic paste in water until tender, then shred.
Cook the wheat and lentils together with salt until completely soft and broken down.
Combine the meat broth, shredded meat, and lentil mixture in a large pot.
Add haleem masala and pound or whisk vigorously to create a thick, blended texture.
Simmer 30 minutes, stirring constantly so the bottom does not catch.
Top with fried onions, ghee tarka, ginger, chilies, lemon, and chopped coriander.

recipes.title: Chicken Karahi
recipes.description: Quick-cooked chicken in a wok with tomatoes, ginger, green chili, and crushed black pepper.
steps: Heat ghee in a karahi or wok over high heat.
Add chicken pieces and sear until lightly browned.
Add ginger garlic paste and saute briefly.
Add chopped tomatoes, salt, crushed coriander seeds, and black pepper.
Cover and cook 20 minutes until tomatoes break down and oil rises.
Uncover and stir-fry on high heat to reduce the gravy.
Top with julienned ginger, slit green chilies, and kasuri methi; serve with naan.

recipes.title: Sindhi Biryani
recipes.description: Spicy, tangy layered rice dish from Sindh with potatoes, prunes, yogurt, and aromatic biryani masala.
steps: Marinate mutton in yogurt, biryani masala, fried onions, and salt for 1 hour.
Cook the marinated mutton with chopped tomatoes and ghee until tender and oil separates.
Fry potato chunks until lightly golden and add to the meat.
Soak rice 30 minutes, then parboil with whole spices and salt to 70 percent done.
Layer rice over the meat, scatter prunes, mint, and remaining fried onions.
Drizzle with saffron water and ghee, then seal the pot and steam (dum) on lowest heat for 25 minutes.
Rest 10 minutes and gently toss before serving with raita.

recipes.title: Seekh Kebab
recipes.description: Spiced minced meat shaped onto skewers and grilled over coals until smoky and juicy.
steps: Combine minced meat with grated onion, ginger garlic paste, chopped chili, coriander, and spices.
Add roasted gram flour and an egg to bind, then knead 5 minutes until tacky.
Refrigerate the mixture for 30 minutes.
Wet hands and shape the mixture around metal skewers into long sausages.
Cook on a hot grill or charcoal, turning every 2 minutes for even color.
Brush with oil halfway through to prevent sticking.
Serve with naan, sliced onion, and mint chutney.

recipes.title: Chapli Kebab
recipes.description: Pashtun-style flat patties of minced beef studded with pomegranate seeds, tomato, and coriander.
steps: Crush coriander seeds and pomegranate seeds coarsely.
Mix minced beef with chopped onion, tomato, chilies, crushed seeds, salt, egg, and cornmeal.
Knead the mixture for 5 minutes until well combined and slightly sticky.
Shape into thin flat patties about the size of a palm.
Heat oil in a wide pan and fry patties on medium heat for 4 minutes per side.
Press lightly with a spatula to keep them flat as they cook.
Serve hot with naan, sliced onion, and lemon.

recipes.title: Chicken Pulao
recipes.description: One-pot rice pilaf with bone-in chicken, whole spices, and a clear, fragrant broth.
steps: Soak rice for 30 minutes.
Boil chicken with one onion, whole spices, salt, and water to make a flavorful yakhni; strain.
Heat ghee, fry sliced onions until golden, then add ginger garlic paste.
Add the cooked chicken and yogurt and saute until oil separates.
Measure the strained yakhni so you have twice the volume of the rice; add to the pot.
Add drained rice and cumin seeds and bring to a boil.
Cover, reduce to lowest heat, and cook 15 minutes; rest 10 minutes off the heat before fluffing.

recipes.title: Sajji
recipes.description: Balochi whole roasted chicken stuffed with rice, marinated only in salt and lemon, slow cooked over coals.
steps: Pierce chicken all over with a knife and rub deeply with lemon, papaya paste, and salt.
Marinate at room temperature 1 hour, then refrigerate 4 hours.
Soak and parboil rice with cumin seeds and salt; toss with oil.
Stuff chicken cavity loosely with the parboiled rice.
Truss legs together and skewer the chicken on a long spit.
Roast over moderate coals for about 1 hour, turning constantly until skin crisps and meat is cooked through.
Serve sliced over the rice with lemon and chaat masala.

recipes.title: Sarson Ka Saag
recipes.description: Slow-cooked Punjabi mash of mustard greens and spinach finished with butter and ghee tempering.
steps: Wash and chop mustard greens, spinach, and bathua.
Pressure cook the greens with chilies, ginger, salt, and a little water for 15 minutes.
Mash the cooked greens with a wooden masher or hand blender until coarse.
Stir in cornmeal slurry and simmer 20 minutes, stirring often.
In a separate pan, heat ghee and fry sliced garlic until golden.
Pour the garlic tempering over the saag.
Top with butter and serve with makki di roti.

recipes.title: Aloo Keema
recipes.description: Spiced minced meat and potato curry, a hearty Pakistani everyday meal eaten with chapati.
steps: Heat oil and fry sliced onions until light golden.
Add ginger garlic paste and cook 1 minute.
Add minced beef and brown, breaking up clumps with a spoon.
Stir in tomatoes, turmeric, chili powder, and salt; cook until tomatoes break down.
Add diced potatoes and a cup of water and simmer covered for 20 minutes.
Uncover and reduce until the masala clings to the meat and potatoes are tender.
Sprinkle garam masala and serve with chapati.

recipes.title: Paya
recipes.description: Slow-cooked trotter stew with rich, gelatinous broth flavoured with whole spices and ginger.
steps: Clean and singe trotters thoroughly, then rinse well.
Heat ghee and fry sliced onions until deep golden, then add ginger garlic paste.
Add trotters and saute 5 minutes, then stir in yogurt and paya masala.
Add 2.5 liters of water and salt, bring to a boil, then simmer 5 hours until completely tender.
Pressure cook for 1 hour as a faster alternative.
Reduce uncovered if needed to thicken the broth.
Garnish with ginger, chilies, and coriander; serve with naan.

recipes.title: Bihari Kebab
recipes.description: Thinly sliced beef marinated in papaya, mustard oil, and roasted spices, then grilled over coals.
steps: Slice beef thinly against the grain into long strips.
Crush fried onions and combine with papaya paste, yogurt, mustard oil, and spices.
Marinate the beef thoroughly and refrigerate 6 hours.
Thread the meat onto skewers in a flat ribbon shape.
Grill over hot charcoal for 3 minutes per side until edges char and meat is just cooked.
Brush with melted butter halfway through.
Serve with paratha, sliced onion, and lemon.

recipes.title: Chicken Handi
recipes.description: Chicken simmered in a clay pot with cream, cashews, and aromatic spices for a smooth, mild gravy.
steps: Heat ghee in a handi and bloom cardamoms.
Add finely chopped onion and saute until soft.
Add ginger garlic paste and cook 1 minute.
Stir in tomato puree and cashew paste and cook until oil separates.
Add chicken and cook until lightly browned.
Whisk yogurt with white pepper and salt and add to the handi over low heat.
Stir in cream, cover, and simmer 15 minutes; serve with tandoori roti.

recipes.title: Falooda
recipes.description: Chilled rose-flavoured dessert drink with vermicelli, basil seeds, jelly, and a scoop of kulfi or ice cream.
steps: Soak basil seeds in water for 15 minutes until they swell.
Boil falooda vermicelli for 5 minutes, drain, and rinse with cold water.
Chop the prepared china grass jelly into small cubes.
Sweeten cold milk with sugar.
In a tall glass, layer rose syrup, basil seeds, vermicelli, and jelly cubes.
Pour cold milk over the layers.
Top with kulfi and chopped pistachios; serve immediately with a long spoon.

recipes.title: Shami Kebab
recipes.description: Pan-fried patties of minced meat and chana dal pounded smooth and bound with egg.
steps: Combine minced beef, soaked chana dal, ginger, garlic, whole spices, chili, and salt with a little water.
Cook covered until meat is fully tender and the water has evaporated.
Cool the mixture, then grind to a smooth paste with chopped onion.
Add egg and knead well; rest for 30 minutes.
Shape into round flat patties.
Pan fry in oil over medium heat until deep golden on both sides.
Serve with mint chutney and paratha.

recipes.title: Chicken Biryani
recipes.description: Pakistani layered rice classic with bone-in chicken, fragrant biryani masala, and slow dum cooking.
steps: Marinate chicken with yogurt, biryani masala, ginger garlic paste, and half the fried onions for 1 hour.
Soak rice for 30 minutes, then parboil with whole spices and salt to 70 percent done.
Cook the marinated chicken with chopped tomatoes and ghee until tender and oil separates.
Layer parboiled rice over the chicken; scatter mint, remaining fried onions, and saffron milk.
Drizzle ghee, then seal the pot and steam (dum) on lowest heat for 25 minutes.
Rest 10 minutes off the heat.
Gently toss before serving with raita and salad.
